21.correct: The Governor has the power to summon, prorogue (end a session of) and dissolve the State Legislature, just as the President does for Parliament.

wrong.B: The Speaker presides over the Assembly's proceedings but cannot summon or prorogue it.
wrong.C: The Chief Minister advises the Governor but does not hold this formal power.
wrong.D: The President exercises this power for Parliament, not for a State Legislature.

21.fact: The Governor summons each session, and the gap between two sessions cannot exceed six months.

22.correct: Disputes arising out of the election of the Vice-President of India are decided by the Supreme Court, whose decision is final.

wrong.A: The President does not adjudicate Vice-Presidential election disputes.
wrong.C: The Election Commission conducts the election but does not settle disputes about it.
wrong.D: Parliament elects the Vice-President but does not judge election disputes.

22.fact: Both Presidential and Vice-Presidential election disputes are heard exclusively by the Supreme Court (Article 71).

23.correct: Maand is a semi-classical folk style of singing that originated in Rajasthan, traditionally associated with royal courts and devotional/romantic themes.

wrong.A: Gujarat's folk traditions include Garba and Dandiya, not Maand.
wrong.C: Haryana is known for Ragini folk songs, not Maand.
wrong.D: Madhya Pradesh has folk forms like Bhagoria songs, not Maand.

23.fact: The famous song 'Kesariya Balam' is a well-known example of the Maand style.

24.correct: To hear a distinct echo, the reflected sound must return after at least 0.1 s; with sound speed about 344 m/s, the obstacle must be at least (344 x 0.1)/2 = 17.2 m away.

wrong.A: 34.4 m is the total path (to and back), not the one-way minimum distance.
wrong.B: 22.5 m does not correspond to the 0.1-second persistence-of-hearing limit.
wrong.D: 10.6 m is too short; the echo would merge with the original sound.

24.fact: The 0.1-second limit comes from the persistence of sound in human ears.

25.correct: Sidney Sheldon's novel 'The Other Side of Midnight' is set against the backdrop of the Second World War and features a tragic love story.

wrong.A: 'Tell Me Your Dreams' deals with multiple personality disorder, not WWII.
wrong.B: 'If Tomorrow Comes' is a con-artist/revenge thriller.
wrong.D: 'Windmills of the Gods' is a political thriller about an ambassador.

25.fact: 'The Other Side of Midnight' (1973) was one of Sheldon's biggest bestsellers and later filmed.

26.correct: India's northern plains are formed mainly from alluvial soil deposited by the Himalayan rivers (Indus, Ganga and Brahmaputra), making them highly fertile.

wrong.A: Black (regur) soil is found mainly on the Deccan trap region, not the northern plains.
wrong.C: Laterite soil forms in high-rainfall tropical uplands.
wrong.D: Red soil occurs over the crystalline rocks of the peninsular plateau.

26.fact: Alluvial soils are divided into the older 'bhangar' and the newer, more fertile 'khadar'.

27.correct: The Brahmaputra river forms Majuli, the world's largest river island, located in Assam.

wrong.A: The Ganga does not form Majuli.
wrong.C: The Yamuna is a tributary of the Ganga and does not form this island.
wrong.D: The Godavari is a peninsular river, unrelated to Majuli.

27.fact: Majuli is a centre of Assamese Vaishnavite (Satra) culture and is shrinking due to river erosion.

28.correct: Per the answer key, the athlete in option C broke the national record by completing the 5000 m race walk in under 20 minutes (19:24.48), a notable youth milestone.

wrong.A: Tushar Panwar is not credited with this particular sub-20-minute record.
wrong.B: Yogita is not the record-setter for this event.
wrong.D: Prashant Kumar is not associated with this 5000 m race-walk record.

28.fact: Race walking is a track-and-field event judged on technique - one foot must appear to stay in contact with the ground at all times.

29.correct: The 'Resolving Insolvency' indicator assesses the time taken, the cost involved, and the recovery rate achieved in resolving insolvency (bankruptcy) cases.

wrong.A: Government debt levels are a fiscal measure, not part of this indicator.
wrong.B: Insurance cover for businesses is unrelated to insolvency resolution.
wrong.D: Loan interest rates are a monetary variable, not what this indicator measures.

29.fact: In India, the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), 2016 sped up and streamlined insolvency resolution.

30.correct: Per the question's framing, the planned Bharatiya Antariksha Station is intended to support a human presence in orbit - sustained station operations and related activities - rather than planetary or lunar missions.

wrong.A: Mars exploration is a separate ISRO objective, not the purpose of the space station.
wrong.B: Lunar mapping is handled by the Chandrayaan missions.
wrong.D: Deep-space communication is supported by ground networks, not the station's purpose.

30.fact: ISRO plans to establish the Bharatiya Antariksha Station (BAS) in stages following the Gaganyaan human-spaceflight programme.

31.correct: In February 2025, ISRO successfully demonstrated ignition of the CE20 cryogenic engine in vacuum conditions at its engine-test facilities, an important capability for upper-stage performance.

wrong.A: The test was a vacuum ignition demonstration, not an in-orbit cryogenic restart.
wrong.C: It was an engine test, not a human-rated life-support test.
wrong.D: Solar-sail deployment is unrelated to this engine test.

31.fact: The CE20 is the indigenous cryogenic engine that powers the upper stage of India's LVM3 rocket.

32.correct: The Indian women's cricket team played its first Test match in 1976 (against the West Indies at Bangalore).

wrong.A: 1973 is before India's women's team made its Test debut.
wrong.C: 1983 is the year India's men won the World Cup, unrelated to the women's Test debut.
wrong.D: 1986 is after the team had already started playing Tests.

32.fact: The Women's Cricket Association of India was formed in 1973, leading to the 1976 debut series.

33.correct: Dr Verghese Kurien is known as the 'Father of the White Revolution' in India for leading Operation Flood, which made India the world's largest milk producer.

wrong.B: Vikram Sarabhai is the father of the Indian space programme.
wrong.C: Norman Borlaug is associated with the global Green Revolution in wheat.
wrong.D: M.S. Swaminathan is called the father of India's Green Revolution.

33.fact: Kurien founded Amul and the cooperative dairy model at Anand, Gujarat.

34.correct: The Industrial Policy Resolution of 1956 classified industries into three categories (Schedules A, B and C), defining the roles of the public and private sectors.

wrong.A: It used three categories, not two.
wrong.C: There were three schedules, not four.
wrong.D: Five is not the number of categories under the 1956 resolution.

34.fact: Schedule A reserved key industries for the State; the 1956 resolution gave the public sector the 'commanding heights' of the economy.

35.correct: Per the answer key, the person in option A was honoured with the Knight Cross of the Order of the Dannebrog in 2025 in connection with strengthening India-Denmark relations.

wrong.B: Rajnath Singh, the Defence Minister, is not the recipient indicated here.
wrong.C: Ashim Kumar Ghosh is not the awardee in this context.
wrong.D: N. Chandrasekaran is not credited with this Danish honour.

35.fact: The Order of the Dannebrog is one of Denmark's oldest and most prestigious orders of chivalry.

36.correct: Under a contractionary fiscal policy the government reduces public expenditure and/or raises taxes to cut aggregate demand, typically to control inflation.

wrong.B: Printing more money is monetary expansion by the central bank, not fiscal policy.
wrong.C: Cutting taxes is an expansionary fiscal measure.
wrong.D: Increasing public expenditure is also expansionary, the opposite of contractionary.

36.fact: Fiscal policy concerns government spending and taxation, while monetary policy concerns money supply and interest rates.

37.correct: After agriculture, the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) sector is the second-largest source of employment in the Indian economy.

wrong.A: Large capital-intensive industries employ relatively few people.
wrong.C: IT and software services are high-value but employ far fewer than MSMEs.
wrong.D: Transport and logistics infrastructure is not the second-largest employer.

37.fact: MSMEs contribute roughly 30% of India's GDP and a large share of its exports.

38.correct: As of 1 March 2025, India had about 32.94 crore active domestic LPG consumers, including 10.33 crore PMUY (Ujjwala) beneficiaries.

wrong.B: 38.94 crore overstates the active-consumer figure cited.
wrong.D: 22.94 crore understates the number of active LPG consumers.

38.fact: The Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) provides free LPG connections to women from poor households.

39.correct: The Santhal rebellion (Santhal Hul) broke out around 1855 (1855-56) in present-day Jharkhand against oppressive moneylenders and British revenue officials.

wrong.A: 1821 is too early for the Santhal rebellion.
wrong.C: 1885 is the year the Indian National Congress was founded, not the Santhal revolt.
wrong.D: 1903 is well after the Santhal uprising.

39.fact: The revolt was led by the brothers Sidhu and Kanhu Murmu.

40.correct: Serampore College, founded by Christian missionaries (the 'Serampore Trio' - Carey, Marshman and Ward), stood on the bank of the Hooghly river in Bengal.

wrong.B: The Yamuna flows through north India, not near Serampore.
wrong.C: The Damodar is a different Bengal river, not the site of the college.
wrong.D: The Mahananda is in north Bengal, not where Serampore College was founded.

40.fact: Serampore College (1818) was among the first institutions in Asia empowered to award university degrees.
